### MSFT is expected to report earnings to $4.23 per share on July 28. Microsoft's fiscal third quarter of 2026, ended March 31, underscores the company's dominance in cloud computing and artificial intelligence amid fierce competition and massive infrastructure investments. As the world's largest software provider, Microsoft relies heavily on Azure growth and AI innovations like Copilot to drive revenue. With shares down over 10% year-to-date prior to earnings due to capex concerns, this report provides critical insight into whether accelerating AI demand justifies escalating spending, influencing not just Microsoft but the broader tech sector's valuation. Microsoft reported total revenue of $82.9 billion for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, up 18% (15% in constant currency) from $70.1 billion a year earlier, exceeding analyst expectations of around $81.4 billion. Guidance for Q4 includes revenue of $86.7-87.8 billion (13-15% growth), Azure growth of 39-40% constant currency, and capex exceeding $40 billion.

# Eaton (ETN) Posts Record Q1 Revenue as Electrical Backlog Surges 48% on Data Center Demand. Eaton Corporation (ETN) delivered a first-quarter record across nearly every financial metric on May 5, 2026, posting revenue of $7.5 billion and adjusted earnings per share of $2.81 — both first-quarter highs for the intelligent power management company. The results came in above the high end of guidance on organic growth and segment margins, reflecting surging demand from data center buildout and sustained aerospace and industrial momentum. Yet despite the beat, Eaton shares fell approximately 3% on the day, as investors focused on near-term segment margin compression tied to the $11 billion in acquisitions closed during the quarter. Eaton reported Q1 2026 revenue of $7.5 billion, up 17% from the first quarter of 2025, driven by three growth components: organic sales growth of 10%, acquisition-related contributions of 4%, and foreign exchange tailwinds of 3% (Eaton Q1 2026 Earnings Press Release, BusinessWire, May 5, 2026).

# Bloom Energy Q1 Revenue Doubling Validates Data Center Power Solution Growth Expectations; Shares Surge Over 16% Post-Market. Bloom Energy reported Q1 2026 non-GAAP revenue of $751 million, up 130.4% year-over-year, beating expectations. The company raised full-year revenue guidance to $3.4 billion–$3.8 billion and expects gross margin around 34% by 2026. During the period, Bloom Energy's non-GAAP revenue reached $751 million, a significant year-over-year increase of 130.4%, surpassing the market expectation of $540 million. Regarding specific business segments, product revenue was recorded at $653 million, a 208.4% increase from $211.9 million in the same period last year and a slight 2.33% increase quarter-over-quarter; installation revenue was $25.931 million, down 22.94% year-over-year; service revenue reached $61.879 million, up 15.56% year-over-year; and electricity revenue was $9.896 million, a year-over-year decline of 63.28%. The most striking data point in this earnings report is the substantial growth in product revenue; this quarter, the company's product revenue surged 208.37% year-over-year to $653 million, accounting for 86.99% of total revenue.

TSMC reported strong first-quarter 2026 financial results, with revenue and profit climbing sharply on continued demand for advanced process nodes. The world’s largest contract chipmaker posted consolidated revenue of NT$1.134 trillion and net income of NT$572.48 billion for the quarter ended March 31. For engineers, semiconductor companies and supply-chain players following manufacturing trends, the results highlight the accelerating demand for leading-edge nodes such as 3nm and 5nm — technologies that underpin AI, high-performance computing and next-generation electronics. “Our business in the first quarter was supported by strong demand for our leading-edge process technologies,” said Huang. “Moving into second quarter 2026, we expect our business to be supported by continued strong demand for our leading-edge process technologies.”. Looking ahead to the second quarter of 2026, TSMC expects revenue to reach between $39.0 billion and $40.2 billion. The outlook suggests that demand for cutting-edge manufacturing — driven by AI and high-performance computing — continues to shape the semiconductor industry’s growth trajectory in 2026.
